narrative.headline: 2022 Toyota 4Runner: suspension and air bags top the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 39 complaints with NHTSA. Suspension and air bags are tied as the most reported areas at 6 reports each (15%), followed by forward collision avoidance and service brakes (5 each, 13%) and steering (4, 10%). No fires or deaths are reported. Air bags stand out: they account for every reported crash (5) and injury (7) in this data, so they are the area worth a close look.

issues.title: Suspension
issues.blurb: Tied for the most reported area: 6 reports (15%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners describe shimmy, shake, or vibration at highway speeds, so feel for that on a test drive.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: Tied for the most reported area at 6 reports (15%), and by far the most serious: 5 crashes and 7 injuries, the only such outcomes in this data. This is the area to check most carefully.

issues.blurb: 5 reports (13%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. A recurring complaint area around the driver-assistance system.

issues.blurb: 5 reports (13%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners describe the brakes engaging on their own in some situations, so test braking behavior carefully.
